summary: The audit for https://moveroo.com.au/sitemap-index.xml scored 62/100. Below is the list of issues requiring attention.

issues.remediation: Add a `<title>` tag inside the `<head>` section of your HTML.
Add a `<meta name="description" content="...">` tag to the `<head>`.
Add `<meta property="og:title" content="...">` for social sharing optimization.
Add `<meta property="og:description" content="...">`.
Add `<link rel="canonical" href="...">` to prevent duplicate content issues.
Add `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">` to the `<head>`.
Add a single `<h1>` tag containing the main topic of the page.
Add JSON-LD structured data to your page. Use a generator or code it manually.
Your schema markup is completely broken. Use a JSON validator to find syntax errors.
Ensure every schema block has a `@type` property defining what it is.
Add the basic properties like `name`, `headline` (for articles), or `url` to your schema.
Add this URL to your `sitemap.xml` file. If using a generator, ensure this page is not excluded.
Your content is too short. Google may consider this "thin content". Add meaningful text, explanations, or details.
Your heading structure is broken (e.g., missing H1, or jumping from H2 to H5). Fix the hierarchy.

Wrap your text content in `

` tags.
Add internal links pointing to other pages on your site.
Critical for E-E-A-T: Add links to Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, and About page.
Add author bylines with links to author bio pages. Use schema.org/Person markup for best results.

issues.issue_detected: 6 words is too thin for general page (recommended: 300+)
issues.remediation: This general needs significantly more content. Aim for at least 300 words.
Add signals across all 3 dimensions: 1. Security (SSL), 2. Legitimacy (Licenses/Awards), 3. Transparency (Reviews/Contact Info).
Ensure your document starts with an `<html>` tag.
We could not detect any ARIA attributes. Review your interactive elements to ensure they are accessible.
Add a link at the very top of your `` that links to your main content id, e.g., `Skip to content`.
Add `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">` to your page head.
Add `twitter:title` and `twitter:description` meta tags.
Add `<meta name="twitter:image" content="...">` to your page.
Add `<meta name="twitter:site" content="@username">` for the site account.
Add `<meta property="og:type" content="website">` (or article, etc).
Add `<meta property="og:image" content="...">` with a 1200x630 image.
Add `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">` to your `<head>`.
Add `<link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/apple-touch-icon.png">` to supports users adding your site to their home screen.

issues.issue_detected: No mobile web app meta tags
issues.remediation: If you want PWA features, add standard mobile web app meta tags.

issues.issue_detected: No compression detected - HTML sent uncompressed
issues.remediation: Enable Gzip or Brotli compression on your web server. For Nginx: `gzip on;`. For Apache: enable mod_deflate.
Add resource hints: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` for third-party origins.
Consider inlining above-the-fold CSS to improve First Contentful Paint.
If you have multiple language or regional versions of your site, add `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="x" href="y" />` tags.
Add an `<h1>` tag containing the main title of the page.
Add a `<link rel="canonical" href="...">` tag to the `<head>`.

issues.issue_detected: Cannot check chain - no absolute canonical URL
issues.remediation: Add an absolute canonical URL first.
Add `<!DOCTYPE html>` as the very first line of your HTML document.
Add `<meta charset="utf-8">` inside the `<head>` section of your HTML.
Your page relies heavily on generic `<div>` tags. Refactor your layout to use semantic HTML5 elements like `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, and `<footer>`.
Ensure your document contains `<html>`, `<head>`, and `<body>` tags properly nested. The `<html>` tag should wrap everything, `<head>` contains metadata, and `<body>` contains visible content.
Add a `<script type="application/ld+json">` block to your page containing your Schema.org data.
Add a clear introduction or "TL;DR" at the top of the page.
Add `Organization` or `Person` schema markup to your home page.
Refactor your HTML to use semantic tags. Wrapping your main content in `<main>` and blog posts in `<article>` is a good start.
Try parsing your content into Q&A format using H2 and H3 tags.
